_schema.description: Ireland trades-vendor coverage. Smallest of the 5 English-speaking markets we cover but with unique mandatory licensing schemes (RGI for gas, RECI/Safe Electric for electrical) and Revenue Commissioners (ROS) compliance for payroll.

vendors.name: MyBuilder.ie
vendors.tagline: Dublin-founded trades marketplace (2010) — same shortlist-pay model as UK MyBuilder
vendors.market_position: MyBuilder.ie is the Irish leg of the MyBuilder network (UK + IE coverage). Free profile, pay only when shortlisted by a homeowner. Strong consumer-side presence in Irish trades search.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Cross-market with MyBuilder UK — same product but Irish-market-tuned for consumer search and trade categories.
pros: Shortlist-pay model — no payment for unsuccessful quotes

vendors.name: Onlinetradesmen.ie
vendors.tagline: Irish-only contractor directory — independent of UK marketplace networks
vendors.market_position: Onlinetradesmen.ie is an Irish-only contractor directory with no cross-market parent. Smaller scale than MyBuilder.ie but operates independently with Irish consumer audience.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish-only independent operator. Smaller consumer audience than MyBuilder.ie but cleaner contractor relationship.

vendors.name: FBD Insurance
vendors.tagline: Irish insurer — €6.5M PL / €13M EL standard, deep agricultural + trades heritage
vendors.market_position: FBD is one of the longest-established Irish insurance companies — originally agricultural insurance, now broad business coverage including tradesman policies. €6.5M Public Liability + €13M Employer Liability standard cover. Strong Irish brand recognition.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish-owned and Irish-focused. PL + EL limits standard are higher than many international alternatives.

vendors.name: AXA Ireland
vendors.tagline: AXA Group's Irish arm — tradesman-specific policy with cross-market consistency
vendors.market_position: AXA Ireland is AXA Group's Irish operation — tradesman-specific policies with the institutional backing of AXA's global insurance footprint. Cross-market with AXA UK + AXA EU.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Cross-market with AXA UK provides corporate consistency for businesses operating across UK + IE.
cons: Pricing typically higher than smaller Irish competitors

vendors.name: BrightPay Ireland
vendors.tagline: #1 Irish payroll software — Revenue-approved, ROS-integrated, free for single-employee co
vendors.market_position: BrightPay Ireland is the #1-recommended Irish payroll software for SMB. Revenue Commissioners-approved with native ROS (Revenue Online Service) integration. Free for single-employee businesses (sole traders). Cross-market with BrightPay UK.
vendors.ie_specific_note: ROS-integrated for PAYE Modernisation real-time reporting (mandatory for IE employers since 2019). PRSI + USC + LPT handling native.
pros: Revenue Commissioners-approved with ROS native integration
cons: Desktop-software model with cloud add-on (not pure SaaS)
Pricing higher than free competitors at scale
Annual licensing rather than monthly subscription
vendors.typical_pricing: Free for 1 employee; €99+VAT/year up to 10 employees

vendors.name: Sage Payroll Ireland
vendors.tagline: Sage's Irish payroll product — €10+VAT/mo, ROS-aligned
vendors.market_position: Sage Payroll Ireland is Sage's Irish-specific payroll offering — €10+VAT/month entry tier with ROS alignment. Strong fit for Irish businesses already on Sage Accounting Ireland.
vendors.ie_specific_note: ROS-aligned for PAYE Modernisation. Same Sage brand recognition that applies UK + IE markets.
pros: Tight bundle with Sage Accounting Ireland
cons: UX dated vs newer Irish entrants
Best value if already on Sage Accounting IE

vendors.name: RGI (Registered Gas Installer)
vendors.tagline: Ireland's mandatory gas-installer registration — unlicensed gas work is illegal under Energy Act 2006
vendors.market_position: RGI is Ireland's official Registered Gas Installer scheme — mandatory for any gas installation, servicing, or modification work in Ireland under the Energy (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006. Unlicensed gas work is a criminal offense. ~3,000 RGI-registered installers operate in Ireland.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish equivalent of UK Gas Safe Register. Registration is mandatory, not optional. Self-certification of work under building regulations available for RGI installers.
pros: Strong consumer trust signal (RGI logo recognized)
cons: No exceptions — every Irish gas worker must be RGI

vendors.name: Safe Electric / RECI
vendors.tagline: Ireland's mandatory electrical contractor registration — legally required for controlled electrical works
vendors.market_position: Safe Electric (Register of Electrical Contractors of Ireland — RECI) is Ireland's mandatory electrical contractor registration. Legally required for any controlled electrical works in Ireland — unlicensed work is criminal. Regulated by Commission for Regulation of Utilities (CRU).
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish equivalent of UK NICEIC for electrical work. Registration mandatory for controlled works (most residential + commercial electrical installation).
pros: Mandatory Irish electrical registration — clear regulatory pathway
cons: Multiple inspection bodies create some administrative complexity

vendors.name: Tradify
vendors.tagline: Simple job-management app for sole traders and small trades teams — popular across Ireland and the UK
vendors.market_position: Tradify is a job-management app built for sole traders and small trades teams — plumbers, electricians, and other tradespeople — and is widely used across Ireland, the UK, Australia, and New Zealand. It focuses on speed and ease: quoting, scheduling, job tracking, and invoicing without heavy configuration, and it integrates with Xero, QuickBooks, and Sage for accounting.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Tradify integrates with Xero and Sage, both common in Ireland, which keeps quoting/invoicing and VAT/accounting connected. It is not Irish-compliance-specific (no native ROS payroll or PAYE Modernisation), so an Irish shop should pair it with an Irish payroll tool like BrightPay.
pros: Fast and easy for sole traders and small teams
Integrates with Xero, QuickBooks, and Sage
cons: Not Irish-compliance-specific (pair with Irish payroll for ROS/PAYE)
Per-user pricing adds up as the team grows
Aimed at small teams, not large or complex operations
vendors.typical_pricing: Per-user monthly (small-team tiers); free trial available

vendors.name: ServiceM8
vendors.tagline: iOS-first job-management app for small trades — low-cost entry and a strong mobile workflow
vendors.market_position: ServiceM8 is an iOS-first (Apple-only) job-management app for small trades — scheduling, quoting, invoicing, and job cards — with a low-cost entry point and unlimited users on paid plans. It is popular with small electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and handyman operators who run a mobile-first workflow, and integrates with Xero and QuickBooks.
vendors.ie_specific_note: ServiceM8 is Apple-only (no Android), so confirm your crew uses iPhones/iPads before committing. It connects to Xero and QuickBooks for Irish VAT and accounting, but it is not Irish-compliance-specific — pair it with an Irish payroll tool for ROS/PAYE.
pros: Low-cost entry with unlimited users on paid plans
Strong mobile-first workflow for small trades
Quick to set up for sole traders and small teams
cons: iOS/Apple-only — no Android (a hard limit for some crews)
Aimed at small operations, not larger or complex firms
vendors.typical_pricing: Low-cost tiers; unlimited users on paid plans; iOS-only

vendors.name: Joblogic
vendors.tagline: Compliance-focused field service management for domestic and commercial trades — strong certificates and PPM
vendors.market_position: Joblogic is an all-in-one field service management platform with strong compliance features — asset registers, planned preventive maintenance (PPM), and certificate workflows — popular with UK and Irish firms doing domestic and light-commercial service work in gas, electrical, and HVAC. It targets the mid-market shop that needs documented compliance, not just scheduling.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Joblogic's certificate and compliance workflows suit Irish gas and electrical contractors who must document certs (and align with RGI/Safe Electric obligations). It is UK/Ireland-focused; confirm Irish VAT/accounting integration and pair it with Irish payroll for ROS.
pros: Strong compliance, certificate, and PPM workflows
All-in-one for domestic and commercial service trades
Established UK/Ireland presence and asset registers
Good fit for gas/electrical/HVAC service firms
cons: More setup than lightweight apps like Tradify or ServiceM8
Mid-market-oriented (more than a sole trader needs)
Not a payroll/ROS solution — pair accordingly

vendors.name: Commusoft
vendors.tagline: All-in-one FSM for service and maintenance trades — strong for gas/heating, used across the UK and Ireland
vendors.market_position: Commusoft is an all-in-one field service management platform built for service and maintenance trades — especially gas/heating, plumbing, and electrical — with job management, scheduling, invoicing, service reminders, and maintenance-contract features. It has a strong UK presence and serves Irish firms doing recurring service and maintenance work.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Commusoft is a strong fit for Irish gas/heating service firms that run maintenance contracts and need service reminders and documented certificates. It is UK-built (not Irish-native compliance), so pair it with Irish payroll/ROS and confirm VAT/accounting integration.
pros: All-in-one for service and maintenance trades
Strong gas/heating fit with maintenance-contract features
Established platform serving UK and Ireland
cons: Comprehensive feature set means more setup
Pricing scales with features and users

vendors.name: Tradesman.ie
vendors.tagline: One of Ireland's largest trades directories — homeowners get up to 3 free quotes from rated tradesmen
vendors.market_position: Tradesman.ie is one of the largest Irish trades directories, connecting homeowners with rated tradesmen who respond with quotes (up to three free quotes per job). The vendor reports 6,000+ active tradesmen providing quotes daily. It is an Irish-only platform with strong domestic consumer-search presence, complementing MyBuilder.ie and Onlinetradesmen.ie.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish-only directory with a quote-request model — homeowners post a job and receive multiple quotes, so tradespeople compete on a shared-lead basis. Strong Irish consumer audience, but expect competition per lead and lighter vetting than UK Checkatrade.
pros: One of the largest Irish trades directories (6,000+ active tradesmen)
vendors.typical_pricing: Lead/quote-based; varies by trade and job value

vendors.name: Arachas (TradeMaster)
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s largest broker, with a trades-liability product built for Irish tradespeople
vendors.market_position: Arachas is Ireland’s largest nationwide insurance broker and runs TradeMaster, a trades-liability product covering Irish tradespeople for 30+ years. It targets carpenters, plumbers, painters and builders specifically, bundling public, employers and products liability with indemnities to Irish trade bodies. As a broker it places cover with multiple underwriters rather than being a single insurer.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Sandyford, Dublin broker; TradeMaster explicitly indemnifies Irish trade/regulatory bodies (RGII, OFTEC, SEAI) and local authorities — strongly Ireland-specific.
pros: Ireland’s largest broker with deep trades specialism
Explicit indemnity to RGII, OFTEC, SEAI and local authorities
cons: Broker model — cover sits with third-party underwriters
Large-broker service can feel less personal

vendors.name: Chill Insurance
vendors.tagline: Irish comparison broker putting tradesman liability cover out to multiple insurers
vendors.market_position: A well-known Irish broker comparing around 14 insurers for business and tradesman cover. It offers public liability for tradesmen from roughly €1.3m upwards plus employers liability, with dedicated tradesman/liability pages. Its strength is breadth of market access and a recognised consumer brand.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Irish-regulated broker operating only in the Irish market; tradesman content framed for Irish trades businesses.
cons: PL limits start lower (~€1.3m base) vs specialists’ €6.5m
Better known for motor/home than trades

vendors.name: KennCo Underwriting
vendors.tagline: 100% Irish-owned underwriting agency with a construction & allied-trades liability product
vendors.market_position: KennCo (founded 2008, Rathfarnham, Dublin) is one of Ireland’s largest underwriting agencies, serving 500+ Irish brokers. It offers a commercial liability product for the construction industry — main contractors and allied trades — with PL indemnity of €6.5m. It is distributed through brokers, not direct to tradespeople.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Dublin-based, 100% Irish-owned, underwriting Irish construction-sector risks through a domestic broker network.
cons: Trades cover sold via brokers only, not direct

vendors.name: Thesaurus Payroll Manager
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s long-established payroll software for small employers and bureaus
vendors.market_position: One of the longest-running, most widely used Irish payroll packages (around three decades), with a large base among small businesses and accountancy practices. Now owned by Bright Software Group (the same group as BrightPay). It is payroll-dedicated, not a general accounting suite.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Fully Revenue-compliant — ROS-enabled real-time PAYE Modernisation (RPN retrieval, submissions, monthly statements), PRSI/USC, SEPA bank files.
pros: Direct ROS / PAYE Modernisation real-time submissions
cons: Bureau tiers needed as headcount grows

vendors.name: Parolla
vendors.tagline: Modern cloud payroll built in Ireland for Irish payroll, on any device
vendors.market_position: A fully cloud-native payroll platform built specifically for Irish payroll (Tramore, Co. Waterford). It won an Irish "Payroll Software of the Year" award (2021) and is a Xero Preferred Partner in Ireland — the most modern, affordable cloud-native option of the Irish-native set.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Built specifically for Irish payroll with a direct ROS connection — auto RPN downloads, one-click submissions, ERR, and auto-enrolment (My Future Fund) integration.
pros: Direct Revenue connection (auto RPN pull, one-click submissions)
Low, transparent monthly pricing + free demo
Integrates with Xero, QuickBooks, Sage One
cons: Per-employee add-on fees rise with headcount

vendors.name: Collsoft Payroll
vendors.tagline: Irish payroll software with a loyal following among small employers — strong in construction
vendors.market_position: A long-standing Ireland-native payroll product with a tiered employer-based licence model, popular with smaller employers and bureaus. It is notably strong in construction thanks to RCT support, and is updated for 2026 pension auto-enrolment. Payroll-focused, not a general ledger.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Fully PAYE Modernisation compliant — submits real-time PSRs to ROS after every run, handles RPNs, PRSI/USC, ERR and SEPA files; updated for 2026 auto-enrolment.
pros: Real-time PSRs to ROS after each run
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~€199/yr (single employer, unlimited employees); Bureau tiers ~€299–499/yr; 60-day trial

vendors.name: Revenue RCT (Relevant Contracts Tax)
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s mandatory withholding-tax system for payments to construction subcontractors
vendors.market_position: RCT is a statutory withholding tax on payments from principal contractors to subcontractors in construction (and forestry/meat processing). Any tradesperson working as a subcontractor must be in the system, because their compliance record sets the deduction rate at source — 0%, 20% or 35%. Poor or absent compliance means 35% withheld, directly hitting cashflow. It is the Irish counterpart to the UK’s CIS.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Administered solely by the Irish Revenue Commissioners; applies only to relevant operations in Ireland; the Irish equivalent of the UK’s Construction Industry Scheme (CIS).
pros: Good compliance record earns the 0% rate
Rolling Bulk Rate Reviews can lower your rate automatically
cons: Cashflow hit (20%/35% withheld if not fully compliant)
Heavy admin for principals (notify every contract + payment)
ROS/eRCT mandatory and complex for sole traders
vendors.typical_pricing: Free (statutory tax regime; all compliance via free ROS)

vendors.name: Construction Industry Register Ireland (CIRI)
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s statutory register of competent builders and providers of building works
vendors.market_position: A national register of builders/contractors established under the Regulation of Providers of Building Works Act 2022 and administered by the CIF. Once the relevant sections commence — a phased rollout during 2026, starting with residential providers — it becomes illegal to carry out building works in a regulated category unless registered. Electrical and gas works are excluded (covered by Safe Electric and RGI).
vendors.ie_specific_note: Ireland-only, under the Regulation of Providers of Building Works Act 2022 (Act 15/2022). Electrical and gas providers are exempt (regulated under Safe Electric / RGI). Register via the CIF.
vendors.reputation_flag: Long-delayed: legislated in 2022 but mandatory registration was still not commenced as of June 2026 (phased rollout during 2026). Treat as a coming requirement, not yet a current legal one — verify live status.
pros: Statutory register backed by primary legislation (future "licence to operate")
Signals competence, insurance and tax compliance
Public register lets homeowners verify a builder
cons: Mandatory status not yet in force (June 2026) — timing uncertain
vendors.typical_pricing: €600 + VAT (€738) to register, plus an annual renewal fee and a €50 online induction

vendors.name: SEAI Registered Contractor
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s mandatory approval list for contractors doing SEAI grant-funded home energy upgrades
vendors.market_position: The official approved-contractor list run by the Sustainable Energy Authority of Ireland. Only registered contractors (or SEAI-registered One Stop Shops) can carry out grant-funded retrofit work — insulation, heating controls, heat pumps, solar. For any tradesperson wanting a share of the large government-subsidised retrofit market, registration is the gatekeeper.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Republic-of-Ireland only, administered by SEAI under Irish grant schemes; solar PV additionally requires being a Safe Electric Registered Electrical Contractor.
pros: Unlocks the large ongoing grant-funded retrofit market
Listed in SEAI’s public contractor search
A pathway into One Stop Shop work
cons: Ongoing compliance burden (valid insurance + tax clearance or removal)
Renewable work needs separate accredited-installer registration
Solar PV adds a Safe Electric REC requirement
Bound to SEAI standards, Code of Practice and inspections
vendors.typical_pricing: Registration fee not published (unverified); ongoing compliance required

vendors.name: Tradesmen.ie
vendors.tagline: Ireland’s largest trade-lead marketplace — homeowners post a job, up to 4 tradesmen bid
vendors.market_position: An Ireland-native, long-established quote marketplace (the "largest of its kind in Ireland") with 6,000+ active tradesmen and jobs in 180,000+ Irish homes. Homeowners post free; the platform texts/emails interested tradesmen, and the first four who respond within 15 minutes get the customer’s details. It is a pay-per-lead marketplace, not a passive directory.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Purpose-built for the Irish market, nationwide. Note this is tradesmen.ie (plural) — distinct from the singular tradesman.ie and from Onlinetradesmen.ie.
pros: Genuine Irish-native presence with a large active base (6,000+)
Vetting (registration fee + references or recognised qualification)
cons: Pay-per-lead credit model (you pay to bid even if you don’t win)
Only the first 4 fast responders get the lead
Bidding inflates cost on high-value jobs
Upfront registration fee + prepaid credits before any return
vendors.typical_pricing: Pay-per-lead via prepaid credits (min bid scales with job value) + registration fee; exact €/credit not public

vendors.name: Bark (IE)
vendors.tagline: Global services marketplace operating in Ireland — pay credits to contact homeowners
vendors.market_position: A large international lead-gen platform with a dedicated Irish presence across plumbers, electricians, builders, drainage and more. It is free for homeowners; tradespeople register free, see matching leads, and spend credits to unlock contact details. Wide reach, but a heavily complained-about lead-quality reputation.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Genuinely runs an Ireland-localised site and sends Irish leads, but it is a cross-market brand (not Ireland-built); the model and complaint pattern mirror its UK/US operation.
vendors.reputation_flag: Strong, well-documented tradesperson warning — fake/unresponsive leads, large sums spent on non-converting credits, hard refunds; aggregate third-party review scores around 2.6/5.
pros: Confirmed live Irish marketplace with broad trade coverage
Free, fast registration; auto-delivered leads by trade/area
cons: Widely documented complaints of poor/fake/unresponsive leads
Pay-per-contact regardless of conversion — costs unpredictable
Multiple pros notified per job → bidding war
Credits expire after 3 months; refunds reported as hard to get
vendors.typical_pricing: Credit-based, pay-per-contact, no subscription; credits valid 3 months; no fixed € pack prices published

vendors.name: Simpro (IE)
vendors.tagline: End-to-end job management and field service software for commercial and residential trades
vendors.market_position: An enterprise-grade field-service platform for established electrical, plumbing, HVAC, fire and facilities contractors. It runs a dedicated "UK & Ireland" operation with local support and has named Republic-of-Ireland customers — one of the most credibly Ireland-present trades platforms. Best for multi-engineer firms, not sole traders.
vendors.ie_specific_note: Operates an explicit "United Kingdom and Ireland" division with local Ireland support and a public Republic-of-Ireland case study; EUR billing handled via the regional operation.
pros: Dedicated UK & Ireland support team (6 days/week)
Verified Irish customers (e.g. Solv, McCarthy Plumbing Group)
Deep workflow (quote → schedule → job → invoice → inventory)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (custom by team size and add-ons)

vendors.name: Eworks Manager (IE)
vendors.tagline: All-in-one job management and field service software for trades and service businesses
vendors.market_position: A mid-market field-service tool covering scheduling, quoting, invoicing, asset/stock and a mobile app for electrical, plumbing, HVAC and maintenance firms. It supports EUR and is distributed into Ireland via the Sage Ireland marketplace with EUR pricing and Sage Irish-accounting integration — a solid workable option for small-to-mid Irish teams.
vendors.ie_specific_note: EUR is selectable on its pricing page, and it is sold into Ireland via Sage’s Irish marketplace with Sage Business Cloud Accounting (Irish edition) integration. Confirm Irish VAT handling at quote.
pros: Listed on Sage Ireland marketplace (~€91/mo) with Sage IE accounting integration
"Pay per team, not per user" model
cons: No dedicated Ireland page on its own site
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~€91/mo via the Sage IE marketplace; team-based tiers (EUR option available)
